informal: Show that if $\prod X_\alpha$ is Hausdorff, then so is $X_\alpha$. Assume that each $X_\alpha$ is nonempty.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_32_2a
  {ι : Type*} {X : ι → Type*} [∀ i, TopologicalSpace (X i)]
  (h : ∀ i, Nonempty (X i)) (h2 : T2Space (Π i, X i)) :
  ∀ i, T2Space (X i)
reasoning: The informal statement says: if the product space ∏ X_α is Hausdorff and each X_α is nonempty, then each X_α is Hausdorff. The Lean signature takes ι-indexed family of topological spaces, hypothesis that each X i is Nonempty, hypothesis that the product Π i, X i is T2 (Hausdorff), and concludes that each X i is T2. This matches the informal statement exactly: same hypotheses (nonemptiness of each factor, Hausdorffness of product) and same conclusion (Hausdorffness of each factor). The quantifier structure (∀ i) and types match.
